ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general black tea beverage class (FOODON_03311737); no specific entry for honey lime black tea exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR covers black tea as a category. No specific code for honey lime sweetened black tea beverage exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227313005 covers black tea as a substance. No specific concept for honey lime black tea beverage exists; closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One large cup (approximately 500 ml / 500 g) as served at KOI Thé, a standard large-size Taiwanese bubble tea shop serving.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, tea, black, brewed, prepared with tap water — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 173228); supplemented with honey nutrient data (FDC ID 169640) and lime juice data (FDC ID 167747) for proportional estimation of sweetened honey lime black tea
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB entry exists for wildflower honey lime black tea as a prepared beverage. Nutrient values are estimated by proportional composition: approximately 85% brewed black tea, 10% wildflower honey (by weight contribution to 100 g of beverage), and 5% lime juice. Energy and carbohydrate are dominated by honey content. Protein and fat are effectively zero from all three components. Sodium is negligible. Sugar reflects honey fructose/glucose content proportionally. Values represent a medium-sweetness commercial preparation typical of Taiwanese tea shops.

gi_source.title: Honey, GI value 55 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283; University of Sydney GI Database entry for honey
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for wildflower honey lime black tea as a beverage. The GI of 55 is assigned based on the dominant glycemic contributor being wildflower honey (GI ~55, low, per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008). Plain brewed black tea has negligible glycemic impact (GI effectively 0). Lime juice contributes minimal carbohydrate. The blended beverage GI is estimated at 55 (low), driven by honey. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving = (55 × 42.5 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 23.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~10 mg per 500 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 2 mg sodium per 100 g (10 mg per large serving), this beverage poses negligible sodium load and is generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension, though the added honey contributes calories that should be accounted for in overall dietary management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content from wildflower honey (~41 g sugar per 500 ml serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: A large serving provides approximately 41 g of free sugars primarily from wildflower honey, with a glycemic load of ~23.4, which is high; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit consumption and consider requesting reduced-sugar or sugar-free alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and sodium; moderate free sugar intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains no fat or sodium of clinical concern, but the high free sugar content from honey (~41 g per large serving) contributes to excess caloric intake and may adversely affect triglyceride levels if consumed regularly, warranting moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wildflower Honey Lime Black Tea (Large) is a sweetened cold tea beverage sold at KOI Thé, a Taiwanese bubble tea chain with outlets in Singapore. It is prepared by brewing black tea and blending it with wildflower honey and fresh lime juice, then served chilled or over ice in a large cup of approximately 500 ml. The drink has a golden-amber colour with a floral sweetness from the wildflower honey, a bright citrus tartness from the lime, and the mild astringency characteristic of black tea. The texture is smooth and liquid with no solids. Nutritionally, the beverage derives almost all of its calories from the honey component, contributing approximately 170 kcal per large serving, with around 42.5 g of carbohydrate predominantly as free sugars (fructose and glucose from honey). Protein and fat content are negligible. Sodium is very low at approximately 10 mg per serving. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low) based on the honey component, with a glycemic load of approximately 23.4 per large serving, making it a high-GL drink. Clinically, this beverage is of concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high free sugar and glycemic load. It is low in sodium and fat, making it less concerning for hypertension and cardiovascular disease from those perspectives, though regular consumption of high-sugar beverages is associated with adverse cardiometabolic outcomes. The drink is dairy-free, gluten-free, and suitable for vegetarians; it is not vegan due to the use of honey. It is a popular menu item in the Taiwanese tea shop category widely available across Singapore.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN) for wildflower honey lime black tea as a prepared commercial beverage. Nutrient values are estimated by proportional composition from USDA SR Legacy entries for brewed black tea, wildflower honey, and lime juice. GI is estimated from the honey component using the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the estimation approach and variability in honey quantity used by KOI Thé.
notes: 1. fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero, as brewed black tea, honey, and lime juice contain no fat of measurable quantity; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for all three components. 2. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are 0.0: naturally zero by composition of ingredients. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero as all components are liquid with no fibre-contributing solids. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are -1: not reported or not reliably quantifiable for this estimated beverage composition from the cited USDA sources. 5. glycemic_index set to 55 (low) based on honey as the dominant glycemic contributor per University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific beverage. 6. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving calculated as (55 × 42.5 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 23.4. 7. Serving size of 500 g used as the standard large (L) cup size for KOI Thé, consistent with Taiwanese tea shop large-cup conventions. 8. The input description '大-野花蜜桔子红茶' confirms large size (大) and wildflower honey (野花蜜) lime (桔子) black tea (红茶). 9. Honey is not vegan; is_vegan set to false. 10. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact ontology entry exists for this specific sweetened honey lime black tea beverage.
